top_signals.title: China's Next Move Could Decide Where Oil Prices Go This Year
top_signals.summary: China’s demand for crude oil imports and the pace of its refined products exports will help shape the trend in oil prices through the end of the year, alongside the supply disruptions in the Middle East.   Decade-low Chinese crude oil imports have eased upward pressure on oil prices in recent months despite the worst supply disruption in the history of oil markets. China’s tumbling demand for crude oil imports was the key demand-side factor in capping the price hikes since the Iran war began.  The market is now increasingly…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Financial Policy Committee Record – July 2026
top_signals.summary: Our Financial Policy Committee (FPC) meets to identify risks to financial stability and agree policy actions aimed at safeguarding the resilience of the UK financial system.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Refiners Sign Direct Contracts with PDVSA for Venezuelan Oil
top_signals.summary: Refiners and oil-producing firms in the U.S. and elsewhere are gaining market share in Venezuela's crude as they sign direct contracts with state-run PDVSA, challenging the global traders that inked earlier deals with the government of interim…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Japan exports and imports in June grow at fastest pace since November 2022, beating estimates
top_signals.summary: Exports climbed 19.3%, while imports spiked 25.4% compared to June last year.
